rule.name: Insecure SharedPreferences for Sensitive Data
rule.rule_desc: Detects usage of SharedPreferences which should not store sensitive secrets.
rule.vuln_desc: Persisting tokens/passwords in SharedPreferences can expose secrets through local extraction or backups.
rule.developer: Use secure storage plugins backed by Keychain/Keystore for secrets.
rule.reviewer: Confirm sensitive values are not persisted in SharedPreferences. This can be confirmed as an issue when the matched path is reachable and required controls are missing, weak, or inconsistent.

rule.name: WebView Unrestricted JavaScript Mode
rule.rule_desc: Detects unrestricted JavaScript execution in Flutter web views.
rule.vuln_desc: Unrestricted script execution in untrusted content contexts can enable malicious interactions and data leakage.
rule.developer: Limit WebView capabilities and enforce trusted origin loading.
rule.reviewer: Review WebView initialization, navigation delegates, and content sources. This can be confirmed as an issue when the matched path is reachable and required controls are missing, weak, or inconsistent.

rule.name: Ignoring TLS Certificate Errors
rule.rule_desc: Detects custom HTTP client callbacks that may bypass certificate validation.
rule.vuln_desc: Accepting invalid certificates allows man-in-the-middle attacks.
rule.developer: Never bypass certificate validation in production; enforce strict TLS checks.
rule.reviewer: Ensure certificate error callbacks do not return permissive decisions. This can be confirmed as an issue when the matched path is reachable and required controls are missing, weak, or inconsistent.

rule.name: Flutter Debug Mode Marker
rule.rule_desc: Detects debug/development mode markers in Flutter apps.
rule.vuln_desc: Debug features in production may expose internals and diagnostics.
rule.developer: Disable debug indicators/features in release builds.
rule.reviewer: Review build profiles and debug-only code guards. This can be confirmed as an issue when the matched path is reachable and required controls are missing, weak, or inconsistent.

rule.name: Flutter Insecure HTTP Usage
rule.rule_desc: Detects cleartext endpoint usage in Flutter apps.
rule.vuln_desc: Cleartext transport can leak credentials and session data.
rule.developer: Use HTTPS only with strict TLS validation.
rule.reviewer: Audit endpoint constants and request builders. This can be confirmed as an issue when the matched path is reachable and required controls are missing, weak, or inconsistent.

rule.name: Flutter Certificate Validation Bypass
rule.rule_desc: Detects custom certificate callback patterns requiring trust validation review.
rule.vuln_desc: Improper callbacks can disable certificate verification and allow MITM.
rule.developer: Avoid permissive callbacks and enforce strict trust checks.
rule.reviewer: Review HttpClient security callbacks and return conditions. This can be confirmed as an issue when the matched path is reachable and required controls are missing, weak, or inconsistent.

rule.name: Flutter WebView JavaScript Unrestricted
rule.rule_desc: Detects unrestricted JS execution in Flutter WebView contexts.
rule.vuln_desc: Unrestricted WebView JS with untrusted content increases injection risk.
rule.developer: Limit JS execution and trust only controlled content.
rule.reviewer: Audit WebView usage and content origin controls. This can be confirmed as an issue when the matched path is reachable and required controls are missing, weak, or inconsistent.

rule.name: Flutter Shared Preferences Secret Marker
rule.rule_desc: Detects local preference storage patterns involving likely sensitive keys.
rule.vuln_desc: Shared preferences are not ideal for sensitive secret storage.
rule.developer: Use secure storage plugins for secrets.
rule.reviewer: Review key names and sensitive data persistence paths. This can be confirmed as an issue when the matched path is reachable and required controls are missing, weak, or inconsistent.

rule.name: Flutter Hardcoded Secret Marker
rule.vuln_desc: Hardcoded secrets can be extracted from source and app binaries.
rule.developer: Externalize and rotate secrets; avoid embedding in app code.
rule.reviewer: Validate no production credentials are committed. This can be confirmed as an issue when the matched path is reachable and required controls are missing, weak, or inconsistent.

rule.name: Android Exported Components in Manifest
rule.rule_desc: Detects exported Android components in Flutter's Android configuration.
rule.vuln_desc: Exported components can expose internal app functionality to external apps.
rule.developer: Export only required components and protect them with permissions where needed.
rule.reviewer: Validate exported component necessity and access controls. This can be confirmed as an issue when the matched path is reachable and required controls are missing, weak, or inconsistent.

rule.name: iOS ATS Disabled
rule.rule_desc: Detects relaxed iOS transport security settings in Flutter iOS projects.
rule.vuln_desc: Relaxed transport policies can expose communication to interception.
rule.developer: Keep ATS enabled and use narrow exceptions only when needed.
rule.reviewer: Review Info. plist transport exceptions and validate domain restrictions. This can be confirmed as an issue when the matched path is reachable and required controls are missing, weak, or inconsistent.
